nativeRange.habitat: Damp riparian zones along streams, rivers, canal banks, and waterlogged soils in temperate wetlands; reported from sea level up to about 500 m elevation

summary: A temperate Eurasian/North American wetland stem plant brought into the hobby as the 'Sao Paulo' and 'Kawagoeanum' cultivars; generally considered a striking background accent that develops magenta-red coloration under strong light and CO2.
careNotes: Persicaria hydropiper is a widespread temperate wetland herb (Polygonaceae) known in botany as water pepper or marshpepper knotweed; the aquarium trade encounters it mainly through two cultivars, 'Sao Paulo' and 'Kawagoeanum', plus the unselected species form. It is a cold-tolerant plant native across Europe, temperate Asia (notably Japan, where it is called tade and used culinarily), North America, Australia, and New Zealand, occurring along stream banks, canal margins, and waterlogged soils. Wild specimens reach 20-70 cm in height; submersed aquarium specimens occupy the same range and are best treated as a background stem plant with strict vertical growth (Flowgrow notes a few isolated stems work better visually than dense groups). Sources broadly agree that good growth requires medium-to-high light (T5/LED), CO2 injection (~20-40 mg/L), and a nutrient-rich substrate with ample iron and phosphate dosing — under those conditions submersed shoots take on intense magenta to wine-red hues, while low-light tanks produce pale green growth and stunted stems. Temperature tolerance is broad (18-30 °C, optimal ~23-27 °C) and reflects the species' temperate origins; the plant is unusually adaptable to cool aquaria and unheated paludariums compared with most tropical stems. pH preference is slightly acidic (5.5-7.0); carbonate hardness is forgiving (0-14 dKH). Propagation is by stem cuttings from the main shoot — replant the trimmed top and the mother stem will branch laterally. PRIMARY-SOURCE NOTE: The designated Buce Plant product page (https://buceplant.com/products/persicaria-sp-kawagoeanum) returned HTTP 404 at research time, so that source was abandoned per playbook and the secondary (Wikipedia) was promoted to primary for taxonomy/native-range data, with Flowgrow added as the husbandry secondary.
breedingNotes: Propagated in the hobby by stem cuttings: trim the top of a mature stem and replant the cutting in nutrient-rich substrate; the mother stem will throw lateral side-shoots. In the wild the species is an annual herb that produces nodding spikes of small four-to-five-tepal flowers and reproduces by seed, but seed propagation is essentially absent from aquarium practice.

primary.name: Wikipedia
primary.notes: Promoted to primary after the designated Buce Plant URL (https://buceplant.com/products/persicaria-sp-kawagoeanum) returned 404. Used for canonical scientific name, family (Polygonaceae), order (Caryophyllales), basionym (Polygonum hydropiper), common-name synonyms (water pepper, marshpepper knotweed, tade), native range across temperate Northern and Southern hemispheres, and wild plant height (20-70 cm).

additional.name: Flowgrow
additional.notes: Cross-checked aquarium husbandry: medium-to-high light, CO2 20-40 mg/L, fast growth, mid/background placement, propagation by stem cuttings, temperature 18-30 °C (optimal 23-27 °C), pH 5-7, kH 0-14, medium care difficulty, iron and phosphate fertilization needs, magenta-red coloration under strong light. Documents the 'Sao Paulo' cultivar specifically — the most widely-traded form of the species in the hobby.
